title: I have around 700 values, can you suggest which column needs to use to apply automation
excerpt:
I have around 700 values, can you suggest which column needs to use to apply automation, based on each and every value automation should happen and the status will be set to a particular value. 
Can you please tell us a …

posts.author: dhawlandra382 (Dhawlandra)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have around 700 values, can you suggest which column needs to use to apply automation, based on each and every value automation should happen and the status will be set to a particular value.
Can you please tell us a detailed flow to achieve the above use case?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dhawlandra382 - if you have 700 options this will need to be held in a separate board and connected to your main board. Would it make more sense to have a status column within the board with the 700 options and when you select it it would show the status rather than setting up 700 automations?

Just a thought,
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: dhawlandra382 (Dhawlandra)
posts.cookedHtml: But which columns support 700 values in monday.com.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
@dhawlandra382 - none of them do, other than a text, numeric or tag column (which are more free form). This is why I suggested putting the 700 values in a separate board and connecting them.

Mark
